# UC-17.1.68 · ISE IoT/OT Device Onboarding — Profile Match and SGT Assignment Validation

> **Criticality:** Medium &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Operations, Compliance &middot; **Wave:** Run &middot; **Status:** Verified

*When an IoT device — a sensor, a camera — joins the network, ISE should put it into the right group. We watch for devices that don't get a group assigned, because they end up with wider access than intended.*

---

## Description

Tracks IoT/OT endpoints whose profile is detected but no SGT is assigned (policy gap) or whose profile-to-SGT mapping appears wrong.

## Value

IoT/OT environments expand rapidly. Endpoints that profile but don't receive an SGT fall through to default policy — usually wider access than intended. Catching the gap allows policy tuning.

## Implementation

Schedule the search every 1 hour over the last 24 hours; alert when an unauthorised IoT endpoint is observed; throttle by MAC for 24 hours.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Connect a new IoT device that profiles into an unmapped category. UC-17.1.68 captures the gap.

### Negative scenario

Connect a known IoT type with a defined SGT. No fire.

## Detailed Implementation

Standard pattern.

## SPL

```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (80001, 5400)
| transaction endpoint_mac maxspan=10m
| stats count by endpoint_type, assigned_sgt
| where isnull(assigned_sgt) OR assigned_sgt=="unknown"
```

## Visualization

(1) Profile-to-SGT matrix; cells without SGT highlighted. (2) Trend of unassigned-SGT endpoints.

## Known False Positives

- Newly added device class — policy lag before SGT defined.

## References

- [Cisco ISE 3.4 — IoT Profiling](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/)
